specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > The Stabiliser Transfer Lemma: A Quantitative Bridge from Memory to Boundary > Meta-Framework Integrations
source_formulae: P0R03143: Layer 9 (the MERA bulk) is the physical location of the agent's deepest, most stable priors. Layer 10 (the boundary) is where these priors are "projected" to generate predictions about sensory input. The lemma is a mathematical certificate that this projection is fault-tolerant. It guarantees that the predictions generated at the boundary are a high-fidelity, error-corrected representation of the agent's core beliefs, ensuring the stability and coherence of the entire inference process. The operational prediction implies that simplifying the predictive model at the boundary can increase its robustness.
test_protocols: preserve Protecting the Priors: source-accounting boundary

source_formulae: P0R03147: The true sigma of an individual is their entire memory holograph in Layer 9. However, for real-time interaction, the Psis field couples to the projection of this memory at the Layer 10 boundary. The lemma guarantees that this projected sigma_boundary is a reliable, error-corrected proxy for the true sigma_bulk.
